what_it_does: This noticeboard item records a design convention/proposal that items should carry an ancestor_path and sibling links so an agent can read context without searching.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id item-43ee78e4, address board://item-43ee78e4, type block, source claude_code, state current.
Title and body heading are 'P9 · --- the context (so the agent doesn't have to search) ---'.
A single links entry of kind part_of points to board://item-e42d651f.
created and updated timestamps are identical.
Body lists two commented fields: ancestor_path and siblings, both using placeholder board:// URIs.
No executable code or symbols are present.
standouts: The title literally frames the item's role as supplying context to avoid agent search.
It proposes ancestor_path and siblings as context fields.
It is linked as part_of a parent noticeboard item.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and markdown body.
Uses board:// URI identifiers for items and relationships.
Uses a P9 prefix in the title, suggesting a numbered pattern or phase marker.
concerns: The ancestor_path and siblings values are placeholders, not resolvable ids.
The item is a stub/proposal with no implementation detail or examples.
created and updated are identical, indicating it has not been revised.
notes: A design note proposing that noticeboard items include ancestor and sibling context links to make them self-contained for agents.
questions: What does 'P9' and the surrounding '---' denote?
Are ancestor_path and siblings intended as formal schema fields or informal annotations?
How would sibling lists be maintained and kept accurate?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to provide context so an agent does not need to search; the actual content only shows placeholder fields for ancestor_path and siblings, so it illustrates the idea without delivering real context.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a pattern where each item carries its own lineage and peer set, reducing the retrieval work an agent must do.
novelty: It treats context provision as a first-class responsibility of the item itself rather than relying on external traversal or search.
contribution: A concrete proposal to embed ancestor_path and sibling references directly in noticeboard items, which a fused system could keep as a context-linking convention.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-43ee78e4 proposes adding ancestor_path and sibling link fields to channel-memory items so agents can read context without searching.
intention_for_embedding: To record a design convention for self-contained context in noticeboard items.
